mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS exceeding 150,000 persons triggers positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures the reinforcing effect of housing-led wealth and retail momentum on equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,350 thousand units, RSAFS at 501,971 million dollars as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with positive wealth effects
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real-time manufacturing and utility output. A sustained 2-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward in anticipation of recession.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. This threshold crossing triggers a negative equity signal over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession probability and corporate earnings downside risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS above 3.0% signals restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations and real discount rates upward, compressing equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.857, FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the ultimate demand signal. Quarters showing real GDP growth acceleration above 2.5% annualized produce positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings growth expectations upward in response to confirmed economic expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,371.854 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both series decline simultaneously over a month, the interaction indicates demand destruction exceeding supply adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006, PAYEMS at 148,912 thousand as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 57% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 18-month window.
